rationale: In the General Discussion, the authors do more than list this reference; they explicitly restate Moreau et al.'s core finding that primary-domain expertise can hinder response to really new products. They use that finding to situate their own work as another individual-level explanation of new product adoption, focused on regulatory focus and risk salience. The cited paper is not used for method borrowing, but it materially informs how the citing paper frames its contribution in the literature.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on prior process-level research represented by Moreau et al. (2001) by adding self-regulation and risk salience as a different mechanism for adoption of new and really new products. It positions itself as a further development of that research stream rather than a contradiction of Moreau et al.'s conclusions.
